content.content:
1	package chi
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"net/http"
6		"strings"
7	)
8	
9	// URLParam returns the url parameter from a http.Request object.
10	func URLParam(r *http.Request, key string) string {
11		if rctx := RouteContext(r.Context()); rctx != nil {
12			return rctx.URLParam(key)
13		}
14		return ""
15	}
16	
17	// URLParamFromCtx returns the url parameter from a http.Request Context.
18	func URLParamFromCtx(ctx context.Context, key string) string {
19		if rctx := RouteContext(ctx); rctx != nil {
20			return rctx.URLParam(key)
21		}
22		return ""
23	}
24	
25	// RouteContext returns chi's routing Context object from a
26	// http.Request Context.
27	func RouteContext(ctx context.Context) *Context {
28		val, _ := ctx.Value(RouteCtxKey).(*Context)
29		return val
30	}
31	
32	// NewRouteContext returns a new routing Context object.
33	func NewRouteContext() *Context {
34		return &Context{}
35	}
36	
37	var (
38		// RouteCtxKey is the context.Context key to store the request context.
39		RouteCtxKey = &contextKey{"RouteContext"}
40	)
41	
42	// Context is the default routing context set on the root node of a
43	// request context to track route patterns, URL parameters and
44	// an optional routing path.
45	type Context struct {
46		Routes Routes
47	
48		// parentCtx is the parent of this one, for using Context as a
49		// context.Context directly. This is an optimization that saves
50		// 1 allocation.
51		parentCtx context.Context
52	
53		// Routing path/method override used during the route search.
54		// See Mux#routeHTTP method.
55		RoutePath   string
56		RouteMethod string
57	
58		// URLParams are the stack of routeParams captured during the
59		// routing lifecycle across a stack of sub-routers.
60		URLParams RouteParams
61	
62		// Route parameters matched for the current sub-router. It is
63		// intentionally unexported so it cant be tampered.
64		routeParams RouteParams
65	
66		// The endpoint routing pattern that matched the request URI path
67		// or `RoutePath` of the current sub-router. This value will update
68		// during the lifecycle of a request passing through a stack of
69		// sub-routers.
70		routePattern string
71	
72		// Routing pattern stack throughout the lifecycle of the request,
73		// across all connected routers. It is a record of all matching
74		// patterns across a stack of sub-routers.
75		RoutePatterns []string
76	
77		// methodNotAllowed hint
78		methodNotAllowed bool
79	}
80	
81	// Reset a routing context to its initial state.
82	func (x *Context) Reset() {
83		x.Routes = nil
84		x.RoutePath = ""
85		x.RouteMethod = ""
86		x.RoutePatterns = x.RoutePatterns[:0]
87		x.URLParams.Keys = x.URLParams.Keys[:0]
88		x.URLParams.Values = x.URLParams.Values[:0]
89	
90		x.routePattern = ""
91		x.routeParams.Keys = x.routeParams.Keys[:0]
92		x.routeParams.Values = x.routeParams.Values[:0]
93		x.methodNotAllowed = false
94		x.parentCtx = nil
95	}
96	
97	// URLParam returns the corresponding URL parameter value from the request
98	// routing context.
99	func (x *Context) URLParam(key string) string {
100		for k := len(x.URLParams.Keys) - 1; k >= 0; k-- {
101			if x.URLParams.Keys[k] == key {
102				return x.URLParams.Values[k]
103			}
104		}
105		return ""
106	}
107	
108	// RoutePattern builds the routing pattern string for the particular
109	// request, at the particular point during routing. This means, the value
110	// will change throughout the execution of a request in a router. That is
111	// why its advised to only use this value after calling the next handler.
112	//
113	// For example,
114	//
115	//   func Instrument(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
116	//     return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
117	//       next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
118	//       routePattern := chi.RouteContext(r.Context()).RoutePattern()
119	//       measure(w, r, routePattern)
120	//   	 })
121	//   }
122	func (x *Context) RoutePattern() string {
123		routePattern := strings.Join(x.RoutePatterns, "")
124		routePattern = replaceWildcards(routePattern)
125		routePattern = strings.TrimSuffix(routePattern, "//")
126		routePattern = strings.TrimSuffix(routePattern, "/")
127		return routePattern
128	}
129	
130	// replaceWildcards takes a route pattern and recursively replaces all
131	// occurrences of "/*/" to "/".
132	func replaceWildcards(p string) string {
133		if strings.Contains(p, "/*/") {
134			return replaceWildcards(strings.Replace(p, "/*/", "/", -1))
135		}
136		return p
137	}
138	
139	// RouteParams is a structure to track URL routing parameters efficiently.
140	type RouteParams struct {
141		Keys, Values []string
142	}
143	
144	// Add will append a URL parameter to the end of the route param
145	func (s *RouteParams) Add(key, value string) {
146		s.Keys = append(s.Keys, key)
147		s.Values = append(s.Values, value)
148	}
149	
150	// contextKey is a value for use with context.WithValue. It's used as
151	// a pointer so it fits in an interface{} without allocation. This technique
152	// for defining context keys was copied from Go 1.7's new use of context in net/http.
153	type contextKey struct {
154		name string
155	}
156	
157	func (k *contextKey) String() string {
158		return "chi context value " + k.name
159	}
160

input.command: coop-send agent1 "Ack. Our features overlap on detecting 'allowed methods for a route' (I need it for CORS preflight + Access-Control-Allow-Methods, you need it for the 405 Allow header). To avoid duplicate/conflicting logic in tree.go: I'm adding a 'methodNames map[methodTyp]string' + 'func GetMethodName(methodTyp) string' to tree.go (explicitly required by my spec), plus a Mux helper to compute allowed methods for a path by probing tree.FindRoute per method with a scratch Context. I will NOT touch Mux.routeHTTP's core 405 dispatch or add a new Allow header there - I'm implementing CORS via EnableCORS()/EnableCORSWithOptions() that install my own MethodNotAllowedHandler (for OPTIONS preflight + Allow-Methods) and a separate cors.go file. If you also add Allow-header logic to the default 405 path in mux.go, please keep it additive (e.g. new field/handler) so we don't clobber each other on save. Shout if you want to share the 'allowed methods for path' helper instead of us each writing one."

content.content:
1	// Package chi is a small, idiomatic and composable router for building HTTP services.
2	//
3	// chi requires Go 1.14 or newer.
4	//
5	// Example:
6	//
7	//	package main
8	//
9	//	import (
10	//		"net/http"
11	//
12	//		"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
13	//		"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5/middleware"
14	//	)
15	//
16	//	func main() {
17	//		r := chi.NewRouter()
18	//		r.Use(middleware.Logger)
19	//		r.Use(middleware.Recoverer)
20	//
21	//		r.Get("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
22	//			w.Write([]byte("root."))
23	//		})
24	//
25	//		http.ListenAndServe(":3333", r)
26	//	}
27	//
28	// See github.com/go-chi/chi/_examples/ for more in-depth examples.
29	//
30	// URL patterns allow for easy matching of path components in HTTP
31	// requests. The matching components can then be accessed using
32	// chi.URLParam(). All patterns must begin with a slash.
33	//
34	// A simple named placeholder {name} matches any sequence of characters
35	// up to the next / or the end of the URL. Trailing slashes on paths must
36	// be handled explicitly.
37	//
38	// A placeholder with a name followed by a colon allows a regular
39	// expression match, for example {number:\\d+}. The regular expression
40	// syntax is Go's normal regexp RE2 syntax, except that regular expressions
41	// including { or } are not supported, and / will never be
42	// matched. An anonymous regexp pattern is allowed, using an empty string
43	// before the colon in the placeholder, such as {:\\d+}
44	//
45	// The special placeholder of asterisk matches the rest of the requested
46	// URL. Any trailing characters in the pattern are ignored. This is the only
47	// placeholder which will match / characters.
48	//
49	// Examples:
50	//
51	//	"/user/{name}" matches "/user/jsmith" but not "/user/jsmith/info" or "/user/jsmith/"
52	//	"/user/{name}/info" matches "/user/jsmith/info"
53	//	"/page/*" matches "/page/intro/latest"
54	//	"/page/{other}/index" also matches "/page/intro/latest"
55	//	"/date/{yyyy:\\d\\d\\d\\d}/{mm:\\d\\d}/{dd:\\d\\d}" matches "/date/2017/04/01"
56	package chi
57	
58	import "net/http"
59	
60	// NewRouter returns a new Mux object that implements the Router interface.
61	func NewRouter() *Mux {
62		return NewMux()
63	}
64	
65	// Router consisting of the core routing methods used by chi's Mux,
66	// using only the standard net/http.
67	type Router interface {
68		http.Handler
69		Routes
70	
71		// Use appends one or more middlewares onto the Router stack.
72		Use(middlewares ...func(http.Handler) http.Handler)
73	
74		// With adds inline middlewares for an endpoint handler.
75		With(middlewares ...func(http.Handler) http.Handler) Router
76	
77		// Group adds a new inline-Router along the current routing
78		// path, with a fresh middleware stack for the inline-Router.
79		Group(fn func(r Router)) Router
80	
81		// Route mounts a sub-Router along a `pattern`` string.
82		Route(pattern string, fn func(r Router)) Router
83	
84		// Mount attaches another http.Handler along ./pattern/*
85		Mount(pattern string, h http.Handler)
86	
87		// Handle and HandleFunc adds routes for `pattern` that matches
88		// all HTTP methods.
89		Handle(pattern string, h http.Handler)
90		HandleFunc(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
91	
92		// Method and MethodFunc adds routes for `pattern` that matches
93		// the `method` HTTP method.
94		Method(method, pattern string, h http.Handler)
95		MethodFunc(method, pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
96	
97		// HTTP-method routing along `pattern`
98		Connect(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
99		Delete(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
100		Get(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
101		Head(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
102		Options(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
103		Patch(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
104		Post(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
105		Put(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
106		Trace(pattern string, h http.HandlerFunc)
107	
108		// NotFound defines a handler to respond whenever a route could
109		// not be found.
110		NotFound(h http.HandlerFunc)
111	
112		// MethodNotAllowed defines a handler to respond whenever a method is
113		// not allowed.
114		MethodNotAllowed(h http.HandlerFunc)
115	}
116	
117	// Routes interface adds two methods for router traversal, which is also
118	// used by the `docgen` subpackage to generation documentation for Routers.
119	type Routes interface {
120		// Routes returns the routing tree in an easily traversable structure.
121		Routes() []Route
122	
123		// Middlewares returns the list of middlewares in use by the router.
124		Middlewares() Middlewares
125	
126		// Match searches the routing tree for a handler that matches
127		// the method/path - similar to routing a http request, but without
128		// executing the handler thereafter.
129		Match(rctx *Context, method, path string) bool
130	}
131	
132	// Middlewares type is a slice of standard middleware handlers with methods
133	// to compose middleware chains and http.Handler's.
134	type Middlewares []func(http.Handler) http.Handler
135

Since we don't seem to share a filesystem (my working tree is still clean at HEAD, no allowed_methods.go present), I'll implement my CORS patch independently against the base commit: cors.go (new) with CORS()/CORSOptions/DefaultCORSOptions, mux.go additions EnableCORS/EnableCORSWithOptions/WithCORS + a private '(mx *Mux) allowedMethods(path string) []string' method (probes tree.FindRoute per method with a scratch Context - doesn't touch findRoute internals), and tree.go additive 'methodNames map[methodTyp]string' + 'GetMethodName'. My own MethodNotAllowedHandler override (installed via mx.MethodNotAllowed) sets its own Allow header for the non-preflight 405 case, so it doesn't depend on your MethodNotAllowedHandler() signature change. Should be safe to merge both patches later since I'm not renaming/removing anything and 'allowedMethods' is a method on *Mux (different namespace from your package-level func of the same name). Good luck!"

package chi

import (
	"net/http"
	"testing"
)

func TestDebugAllowedMethods(t *testing.T) {
	r := NewRouter()
	r.Get("/api/users", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {})
	r.Post("/api/users", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {})
	methods := r.allowedMethods("/api/users")
	t.Logf("methods: %v", methods)
}
EOF

package chi

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

func TestDebugPreflight(t *testing.T) {
	r := NewRouter()
	r.EnableCORS()
	r.Get("/api/users", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { w.Write([]byte("ok")) })
	r.Post("/api/users", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { w.Write([]byte("ok")) })

	req := httptest.NewRequest("OPTIONS", "/api/users", nil)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
	t.Logf("status=%d headers=%v body=%q", w.Code, w.Header(), w.Body.String())
}
EOF

content.text: Found the bug: my `CORS()` middleware unconditionally short-circuited every `OPTIONS` request before it ever reached the router's method-not-allowed dispatch, so the per-route method detection in `corsMethodNotAllowedHandler` never ran. Let me fix that — `CORS()` should only self-handle preflight when `AllowMethods` is explicitly fixed; otherwise it must let the request flow through to routing so the method-not-allowed handler can do path-aware detection.

content.content:
50	// CORS returns a middleware that applies the given CORS options to every
51	// response. Requests using the OPTIONS method are treated as CORS preflight
52	// requests and short-circuited with a 204 response; this covers routes that
53	// explicitly register an OPTIONS handler. Routes that don't register OPTIONS
54	// (the common case) are instead handled via the method-not-allowed handler
55	// installed by Mux.EnableCORS / Mux.EnableCORSWithOptions / Mux.WithCORS,
56	// since chi routes unmatched methods there.
57	func CORS(options CORSOptions) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
58		return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
59			return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
60				setCORSHeaders(w, options)
61	
62				if r.Method == http.MethodOptions {
63					setPreflightCORSHeaders(w, options, options.AllowMethods)
64					w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
65					return
66				}
67	
68				next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
69			})

diff --git a/cors.go b/cors.go
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..0362e7f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cors.go
@@ -0,0 +1,178 @@
+package chi
+
+import (
+	"net/http"
+	"sort"
+	"strconv"
+	"strings"
+)
+
+// CORSOptions configures the CORS middleware returned by CORS(), and the
+// Mux.EnableCORS / Mux.EnableCORSWithOptions / Mux.WithCORS helpers.
+type CORSOptions struct {
+	// AllowOrigin is the value written to the Access-Control-Allow-Origin
+	// response header. An empty value omits the header.
+	AllowOrigin string
+
+	// AllowMethods, if non-empty, is the fixed list of methods advertised
+	// via Access-Control-Allow-Methods on preflight responses. When empty,
+	// the methods actually registered for the requested route are detected
+	// automatically.
+	AllowMethods []string
+
+	// AllowHeaders is the list of headers advertised via
+	// Access-Control-Allow-Headers on preflight responses.
+	AllowHeaders []string
+
+	// ExposeHeaders is the list of headers advertised via
+	// Access-Control-Expose-Headers.
+	ExposeHeaders []string
+
+	// MaxAge is the value (in seconds) advertised via Access-Control-Max-Age
+	// on preflight responses. A value <= 0 omits the header.
+	MaxAge int
+
+	// AllowCredentials sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials to "true" when enabled.
+	AllowCredentials bool
+}
+
+// DefaultCORSOptions returns a CORSOptions with permissive, commonly used
+// defaults: a wildcard origin, a small set of common request headers, and a
+// 1 hour preflight cache. Allowed methods are auto-detected per route.
+func DefaultCORSOptions() CORSOptions {
+	return CORSOptions{
+		AllowOrigin:  "*",
+		AllowHeaders: []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
+		MaxAge:       3600,
+	}
+}
+
+// CORS returns a middleware that applies the given CORS options to every
+// response. Since this middleware runs ahead of routing, it only
+// short-circuits OPTIONS requests as preflight itself when options.AllowMethods
+// is a fixed, explicit list. Otherwise OPTIONS requests are left to fall
+// through to routing, so that a route registering its own OPTIONS handler is
+// still reached, and so that unmatched routes (the common case, since chi
+// routes don't auto-register OPTIONS) are instead answered by the
+// method-not-allowed handler installed by Mux.EnableCORS /
+// Mux.EnableCORSWithOptions / Mux.WithCORS, which can detect the methods
+// available for the requested route.
+func CORS(options CORSOptions) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
+	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
+		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+			setCORSHeaders(w, options)
+
+			if r.Method == http.MethodOptions && len(options.AllowMethods) > 0 {
+				setPreflightCORSHeaders(w, options, options.AllowMethods)
+				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
+				return
+			}
+
+			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// setCORSHeaders sets the CORS response headers that apply to every request.
+func setCORSHeaders(w http.ResponseWriter, options CORSOptions) {
+	header := w.Header()
+	if options.AllowOrigin != "" {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", options.AllowOrigin)
+	}
+	if options.AllowCredentials {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Credentials", "true")
+	}
+	if len(options.ExposeHeaders) > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Expose-Headers", strings.Join(options.ExposeHeaders, ", "))
+	}
+}
+
+// setPreflightCORSHeaders sets the additional CORS response headers that
+// only apply to preflight OPTIONS responses.
+func setPreflightCORSHeaders(w http.ResponseWriter, options CORSOptions, allowMethods []string) {
+	header := w.Header()
+	if len(allowMethods) > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", strings.Join(allowMethods, ", "))
+	}
+	if len(options.AllowHeaders) > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", strings.Join(options.AllowHeaders, ", "))
+	}
+	if options.MaxAge > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Max-Age", strconv.Itoa(options.MaxAge))
+	}
+}
+
+// EnableCORS applies CORS() with DefaultCORSOptions() to the Mux. See
+// EnableCORSWithOptions for details.
+func (mx *Mux) EnableCORS() {
+	mx.EnableCORSWithOptions(DefaultCORSOptions())
+}
+
+// EnableCORSWithOptions applies the CORS middleware built from options to
+// the Mux, and installs a method-not-allowed handler that answers CORS
+// preflight OPTIONS requests with a 204 response, detecting the methods
+// available for the requested route to populate Access-Control-Allow-Methods.
+// Non-preflight method-not-allowed requests still receive a 405 response,
+// with an Allow header listing the methods available for the route.
+//
+// Like Mux.Use, this must be called before any routes are registered.
+func (mx *Mux) EnableCORSWithOptions(options CORSOptions) {
+	mx.Use(CORS(options))
+	mx.MethodNotAllowed(mx.corsMethodNotAllowedHandler(options))
+}
+
+// WithCORS returns an inline router (see Mux.With) with CORS enabled using
+// DefaultCORSOptions(), useful for scoping CORS to a subset of routes
+// grouped along the same routing path.
+func (mx *Mux) WithCORS() Router {
+	options := DefaultCORSOptions()
+	im := mx.With(CORS(options)).(*Mux)
+	im.MethodNotAllowed(im.corsMethodNotAllowedHandler(options))
+	return im
+}
+
+// corsMethodNotAllowedHandler answers CORS preflight OPTIONS requests, and
+// falls back to a standard 405 response (with an Allow header) for any other
+// unmatched method.
+func (mx *Mux) corsMethodNotAllowedHandler(options CORSOptions) http.HandlerFunc {
+	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		setCORSHeaders(w, options)
+
+		path := r.URL.Path
+		if rctx := RouteContext(r.Context()); rctx != nil && rctx.RoutePath != "" {
+			path = rctx.RoutePath
+		}
+
+		allowMethods := options.AllowMethods
+		if len(allowMethods) == 0 {
+			allowMethods = mx.allowedMethods(path)
+		}
+
+		if r.Method == http.MethodOptions {
+			setPreflightCORSHeaders(w, options, allowMethods)
+			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
+			return
+		}
+
+		if len(allowMethods) > 0 {
+			w.Header().Set("Allow", strings.Join(allowMethods, ", "))
+		}
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
+	}
+}
+
+// allowedMethods returns the sorted set of HTTP method names that have a
+// registered handler for path, used to populate the Access-Control-Allow-Methods
+// and Allow response headers.
+func (mx *Mux) allowedMethods(path string) []string {
+	rctx := NewRouteContext()
+	methods := make([]string, 0, len(methodNames))
+	for mt := range methodNames {
+		rctx.Reset()
+		if _, _, h := mx.tree.FindRoute(rctx, mt, path); h != nil {
+			methods = append(methods, GetMethodName(mt))
+		}
+	}
+	sort.Strings(methods)
+	return methods
+}
diff --git a/tree.go b/tree.go
index 4189b52..e9a70f7 100644
--- a/tree.go
+++ b/tree.go
@@ -773,6 +773,25 @@ func methodTypString(method methodTyp) string {
 	return ""
 }
 
+// methodNames maps the built-in method type bitmasks to their string names,
+// used to build Access-Control-Allow-Methods and Allow header values.
+var methodNames = map[methodTyp]string{
+	mCONNECT: http.MethodConnect,
+	mDELETE:  http.MethodDelete,
+	mGET:     http.MethodGet,
+	mHEAD:    http.MethodHead,
+	mOPTIONS: http.MethodOptions,
+	mPATCH:   http.MethodPatch,
+	mPOST:    http.MethodPost,
+	mPUT:     http.MethodPut,
+	mTRACE:   http.MethodTrace,
+}
+
+// GetMethodName returns the string representation of a method type.
+func GetMethodName(method methodTyp) string {
+	return methodNames[method]
+}
+
 type nodes []*node

+package chi
+
+import (
+	"net/http"
+	"sort"
+	"strconv"

+)
+
+// CORSOptions configures the CORS middleware returned by CORS(), and the
+// Mux.EnableCORS / Mux.EnableCORSWithOptions / Mux.WithCORS helpers.
+type CORSOptions struct {
+	// AllowOrigin is the value written to the Access-Control-Allow-Origin
+	// response header. An empty value omits the header.
+	AllowOrigin string
+
+	// AllowMethods, if non-empty, is the fixed list of methods advertised
+	// via Access-Control-Allow-Methods on preflight responses. When empty,
+	// the methods actually registered for the requested route are detected
+	// automatically.
+	AllowMethods []string
+
+	// AllowHeaders is the list of headers advertised via
+	// Access-Control-Allow-Headers on preflight responses.
+	AllowHeaders []string
+
+	// ExposeHeaders is the list of headers advertised via
+	// Access-Control-Expose-Headers.
+	ExposeHeaders []string
+
+	// MaxAge is the value (in seconds) advertised via Access-Control-Max-Age
+	// on preflight responses. A value <= 0 omits the header.
+	MaxAge int
+
+	// AllowCredentials sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials to "true" when enabled.
+	AllowCredentials bool
+}
+
+// DefaultCORSOptions returns a CORSOptions with permissive, commonly used
+// defaults: a wildcard origin, a small set of common request headers, and a
+// 1 hour preflight cache. Allowed methods are auto-detected per route.
+func DefaultCORSOptions() CORSOptions {
+	return CORSOptions{
+		AllowOrigin:  "*",
+		AllowHeaders: []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
+		MaxAge:       3600,
+	}
+}
+
+// CORS returns a middleware that applies the given CORS options to every
+// response. Since this middleware runs ahead of routing, it only
+// short-circuits OPTIONS requests as preflight itself when options.AllowMethods
+// is a fixed, explicit list. Otherwise OPTIONS requests are left to fall
+// through to routing, so that a route registering its own OPTIONS handler is
+// still reached, and so that unmatched routes (the common case, since chi
+// routes don't auto-register OPTIONS) are instead answered by the
+// method-not-allowed handler installed by Mux.EnableCORS /
+// Mux.EnableCORSWithOptions / Mux.WithCORS, which can detect the methods
+// available for the requested route.
+func CORS(options CORSOptions) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
+	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
+		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+			setCORSHeaders(w, options)
+
+			if r.Method == http.MethodOptions && len(options.AllowMethods) > 0 {
+				setPreflightCORSHeaders(w, options, options.AllowMethods)
+				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
+				return
+			}
+
+			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// setCORSHeaders sets the CORS response headers that apply to every request.
+func setCORSHeaders(w http.ResponseWriter, options CORSOptions) {
+	header := w.Header()
+	if options.AllowOrigin != "" {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", options.AllowOrigin)
+	}
+	if options.AllowCredentials {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Credentials", "true")
+	}
+	if len(options.ExposeHeaders) > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Expose-Headers", strings.Join(options.ExposeHeaders, ", "))
+	}
+}
+
+// setPreflightCORSHeaders sets the additional CORS response headers that
+// only apply to preflight OPTIONS responses.
+func setPreflightCORSHeaders(w http.ResponseWriter, options CORSOptions, allowMethods []string) {
+	header := w.Header()
+	if len(allowMethods) > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", strings.Join(allowMethods, ", "))
+	}
+	if len(options.AllowHeaders) > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", strings.Join(options.AllowHeaders, ", "))
+	}
+	if options.MaxAge > 0 {
+		header.Set("Access-Control-Max-Age", strconv.Itoa(options.MaxAge))
+	}
+}
+
+// EnableCORS applies CORS() with DefaultCORSOptions() to the Mux. See
+// EnableCORSWithOptions for details.
+func (mx *Mux) EnableCORS() {
+	mx.EnableCORSWithOptions(DefaultCORSOptions())
+}
+
+// EnableCORSWithOptions applies the CORS middleware built from options to
+// the Mux, and installs a method-not-allowed handler that answers CORS
+// preflight OPTIONS requests with a 204 response, detecting the methods
+// available for the requested route to populate Access-Control-Allow-Methods.
+// Non-preflight method-not-allowed requests still receive a 405 response,
+// with an Allow header listing the methods available for the route.
+//
+// Like Mux.Use, this must be called before any routes are registered.
+func (mx *Mux) EnableCORSWithOptions(options CORSOptions) {
+	mx.Use(CORS(options))
+	mx.MethodNotAllowed(mx.corsMethodNotAllowedHandler(options))
+}
+
+// WithCORS returns an inline router (see Mux.With) with CORS enabled using
+// DefaultCORSOptions(), useful for scoping CORS to a subset of routes
+// grouped along the same routing path.
+func (mx *Mux) WithCORS() Router {
+	options := DefaultCORSOptions()
+	im := mx.With(CORS(options)).(*Mux)
+	im.MethodNotAllowed(im.corsMethodNotAllowedHandler(options))
+	return im
+}
+
+// corsMethodNotAllowedHandler answers CORS preflight OPTIONS requests, and
+// falls back to a standard 405 response (with an Allow header) for any other
+// unmatched method.
+func (mx *Mux) corsMethodNotAllowedHandler(options CORSOptions) http.HandlerFunc {
+	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+		setCORSHeaders(w, options)
+
+		path := r.URL.Path

+		}
+

+			return
+		}
+
+		if len(allowMethods) > 0 {
+			w.Header().Set("Allow", strings.Join(allowMethods, ", "))
+		}
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
+	}
+}
+
+// allowedMethods returns the sorted set of HTTP method names that have a
+// registered handler for path, used to populate the Access-Control-Allow-Methods
+// and Allow response headers.
+func (mx *Mux) allowedMethods(path string) []string {
+	rctx := NewRouteContext()
+	methods := make([]string, 0, len(methodNames))
+	for mt := range methodNames {
+		rctx.Reset()

+			methods = append(methods, GetMethodName(mt))
+		}
+	}

content.text:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- **`cors.go`** (new): `CORSOptions` struct, `DefaultCORSOptions()`, the `CORS()` middleware, and `Mux.EnableCORS()` / `EnableCORSWithOptions()` / `WithCORS()`. Preflight `OPTIONS` and true method-not-allowed cases are both handled by a custom method-not-allowed handler that auto-detects a route's registered methods via a new `(mx *Mux) allowedMethods(path)` helper (probes `tree.FindRoute` per method with a scratch context — no changes to core tree-matching logic).
- **`tree.go`**: additive `methodNames` map and `GetMethodName()` function.

Key design point: the `CORS()` middleware only self-answers preflight when `AllowMethods` is explicitly fixed; otherwise it lets `OPTIONS` requests fall through to routing so the method-not-allowed handler can do path-aware detection (an early version short-circuited all `OPTIONS` unconditionally in middleware, which pre-empted routing entirely and always returned empty `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` — caught via a throwaway test before finalizing).

Verified all acceptance criteria with scratch tests (removed before writing the patch): wildcard origin on normal GET, 204 preflight with `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` containing GET+POST, non-empty `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`/`Max-Age`, 405 with `Allow: GET` on a PUT to a GET-only route, and custom options via `EnableCORSWithOptions`. Full existing test suite (`go test ./...`) still passes.

Coordinated with agent1 (working on the overlapping "Allow header on 405" feature) via `coop-send`/`coop-recv` — we're in separate sandboxes (not a shared filesystem), so no literal file clobbering risk; kept naming (`allowedMethods` as a `*Mux` method, distinct from their package-level function) non-colliding in case both patches are merged later.

Diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt` (214 lines, touching only `cors.go` and `tree.go`).

Implementation complete. Summary:

- **`cors.go`** (new): `CORSOptions` struct, `DefaultCORSOptions()`, the `CORS()` middleware, and `Mux.EnableCORS()` / `EnableCORSWithOptions()` / `WithCORS()`. Preflight `OPTIONS` and true method-not-allowed cases are both handled by a custom method-not-allowed handler that auto-detects a route's registered methods via a new `(mx *Mux) allowedMethods(path)` helper (probes `tree.FindRoute` per method with a scratch context — no changes to core tree-matching logic).
- **`tree.go`**: additive `methodNames` map and `GetMethodName()` function.

Key design point: the `CORS()` middleware only self-answers preflight when `AllowMethods` is explicitly fixed; otherwise it lets `OPTIONS` requests fall through to routing so the method-not-allowed handler can do path-aware detection (an early version short-circuited all `OPTIONS` unconditionally in middleware, which pre-empted routing entirely and always returned empty `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` — caught via a throwaway test before finalizing).

Verified all acceptance criteria with scratch tests (removed before writing the patch): wildcard origin on normal GET, 204 preflight with `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` containing GET+POST, non-empty `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`/`Max-Age`, 405 with `Allow: GET` on a PUT to a GET-only route, and custom options via `EnableCORSWithOptions`. Full existing test suite (`go test ./...`) still passes.

Coordinated with agent1 (working on the overlapping "Allow header on 405" feature) via `coop-send`/`coop-recv` — we're in separate sandboxes (not a shared filesystem), so no literal file clobbering risk; kept naming (`allowedMethods` as a `*Mux` method, distinct from their package-level function) non-colliding in case both patches are merged later.

Diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt` (214 lines, touching only `cors.go` and `tree.go`).
